member.name: Sir Mark Hendrick
member.synopsis: Sir Mark Hendrick is the Labour (Co-op) MP for Preston , and has been an MP continually since 23 November 2000.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Civil Servants: Workplace Pensions
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, what steps his Department is taking to ensure that people with advanced and terminal illnesses receive the Civil Service Pensions in a timely manner.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Cabinet Office awarded Capita the contract to administer the Civil Service Pension Scheme in November 2023 under the previous government. The current delays facing scheme members are entirely unacceptable, and this Government has taken firm action to ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Cabinet Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Mental Health Services: Young People
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what assessment he has made of the (a) adequacy of mental health provision for young people and (b) the potential impact of rates of anxiety, depression, autism and ADHD on youth economic inactivit
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is committed to improving access to mental health services for children and young people, and to ensuring a system that is simpler, faster, and stronger at every stage. Access to support is expanding, but services remain under pressure, and...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Recruitment: Young People
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, what steps the Government is taking to help ensure that automated and remote recruitment processes do not act as a barrier to entry-level employment for young people with no prior work
writtenQuestions.answerText: Most AI systems are already regulated at the point of use by our existing expert regulators. The ICO has recently closed a consultation on their draft guidance about automated decision-making, including profiling. Government has previously published Respo...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: UK Relations with EU: Pollution Control
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, if he will make it his policy to align Government policy with the EU’s higher air quality standards as part of the European Partnership Bill.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The European Partnership Bill will provide the necessary powers to deliver treaties with the European Union now and in the future. Parliament will have the opportunity to scrutinise the Bill when it is introduced to Parliament in the usual way.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Cabinet Office

writtenQuestions.heading: UK Relations with EU: Import Controls
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, if the European Partnership Bill will include provisions on Sanitary and Phytosanitary (SPS) standards.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The European Partnership Bill will provide the necessary powers to deliver treaties with the European Union now and in the future. Parliament will have the opportunity to scrutinise the Bill when it is introduced to Parliament in the usual way.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Cabinet Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Prostate Cancer: Diagnosis
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, whether his Department plans to introduce the Stockholm3 test for prostate cancer within the NHS.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the Hon. Member to the answer I gave to the Hon. Member for Ceredigion Preseli on 26 May 2026 to Question 1677.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Asylum: Afghanistan
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, if she will make it her policy to expedite the processing of asylum claims of Afghan nationals who (a) served with and (b) assisted British troops in the Afghanistan war.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Every asylum claim admitted to the UK asylum system is carefully considered on its individual merits, in line with published policies. There are clear processes in place for prioritising asylum claims on the basis of vulnerability in line with our publish...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Home Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Offences against Children: Social Media
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, what steps she is taking steps to prevent and restrict social media sites from being used for child sex trafficking activity.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Child sexual exploitation and abuse (CSEA) is an abhorrent and serious crime and tackling it online is an absolute priority for this Government. The Online Safety Act places its strongest protections around children, imposing robust legal duties on online...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Biofuels: Regulation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what steps his Department is taking to strengthen regulations on biomass energy production and waste incineration companies, including to ensure the sector takes responsibility for reducing i
writtenQuestions.answerText: Biomass electricity generators receiving support must comply with strict sustainability criteria covering supply chain greenhouse gas emissions. The government is developing a cross-sector biomass sustainability framework to further strengthen these requi...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Heat Pumps
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what assessment he has made of trends in the level of rollout of heat pumps by boiler manufacturers.
writtenQuestions.answerText: UK heat pump sales hit a record high in 2025, and the government expects to see continued growth over the coming years. Manufacturers of a range of heating technologies, including fossil fuel boilers, are among the leading players in this growing market. ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Agricultural Products: Prices
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs, what steps her Department is taking to help tackle inflation in the cost of produce caused by increased energy costs due to the conflict in Iran.
writtenQuestions.answerText: It has not proved possible to respond to the hon. Member in the time available before Prorogation.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

writtenQuestions.heading: Cybercrime and Cybersecurity
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, what assessment her Department has made of the proportion of companies paying ransoms to hackers following cyber-attacks; and what steps her Department is taking to improve cybersecurit
writtenQuestions.answerText: The government considers ransomware a serious threat to the UK’s national security and economic prosperity. The Home Office recently consulted on a package of proposals to reduce the threat ransomware poses to the UK economy and will continue to develop t...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Cybercrime: Artificial Intelligence
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what assessment she has made of the increasing use of artificial intelligence by criminals in conducting cyberattacks.
writtenQuestions.answerText: As the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology and the Security Minister set out in their letter to businesses on 15 April 2026, the cyber threat continues to change. A new generation of AI models are becoming increasingly capable at an ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Home Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Artificial Intelligence: Energy
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, what assessment she has made of the impact of rising energy prices on the AI sector in the United Kingdom.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We are focused on continuing to create the right conditions for investment in the UK’s AI and data centre infrastructure.Through the AI Energy Council, we are already bringing together energy system bodies and leading technology companies, including NESO,...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Gambling: Licensing
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, what steps her Department is taking to help tackle illegal online sport gambling sites that do not hold a licence to operate in the UK.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Since April 2024, the Gambling Commission has significantly increased its disruption activity and has focused on finding innovative ways to tackle the illegal market. The Crime and Policing Bill, once passed, will give the Gambling Commission greater powe...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Culture, Media and Sport

writtenQuestions.heading: Basketball: Facilities
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, what assessment her Department has made of the adequacy of the (a) condition and (b) availability of outdoor basketball courts; and what steps her Department is taking to ensure adequate funding
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is committed to ensuring that everyone, regardless of background, should have access to and benefit from quality sport and physical activity opportunities. This means delivering a range of facilities across the country based on what each lo...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Culture, Media and Sport

writtenQuestions.heading: Police: Biometrics
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what steps she is taking to minimise the risk of racial bias found in AI powered Live Facial Recognition systems.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Police forces using facial recognition must comply with existing legal obligations, including the Human Rights Act 1998, Equality Act 2010 and Data Protection Act 2018.Facial recognition algorithms provided by or procured with Home Office funding for poli...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Home Office

writtenQuestions.heading: Natural Gas
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what steps he is taking to (a) expand and (b) ensure resiliency of the UK’s gas (i) infrastructure and storage systems and (ii) reserves.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Government works continuously with industry to improve and maintain the resilience and security of energy infrastructure to a range of evolving risks and future system changes. In 2026, DESNZ will publish an Energy Resilience Strategy to set out Governmen...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Renewable Energy: Employment
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what steps he is taking to ensure that the green workforce is expanding at a pace sufficient to meet the growing demands of an accelerated energy transition.
writtenQuestions.answerText: In October 2025, the Government published the Clean Energy Jobs Plan (CEJP). The CEJP sets out the workforce needed to deliver our clean energy superpower mission, and how the government will work in partnership with industry and trade unions to deliver i...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Schools: Lancashire
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Education, what capital and revenue funding allocations have been made to local authorities in Lancashire for a) primary schools, b) secondary schools and c) specialist school places over the next 2 years.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Revenue funding through the Dedicated Schools Grant (DSG) for Lancashire in 2025/26 and 2026/27 financial years are presented in the table below. Funding for 2027/28 will be confirmed later this year.Lancashire School FundingFinancial Year2025/262026/27Ov...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Education
